verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is explicitly described as a soluble protein with no transmembrane domain, in contrast to IZUMO1/2/3 which are TM proteins. In sperm it is intra-acrosomal (not surface-exposed), and in somatic tissues it is cytoplasmic or nuclear (Human Protein Atlas: cytoplasmic in seminiferous duct cells; GeneCards/Alliance: nuclear). Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program exists; (2) not a sheddase substrate — no TM isoform described; (3) no covalent or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering documented; (4) no PM minority pool or secretory cycling reported; (5) five contextual buckets: cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/ICD/activation outer-leaflet ecto-pool; tissue_restricted_surface — no TM or GPI anchor in any lineage; lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; dual_localization — no documented PM pool; stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. All buckets ruled out.
